subject: Social Science
learning_outcomes: Explain the meaning and importance of public finance
Explain the concept and importance of budget
Understand the relevance of deficit finance

key_terms.definition: Finance of the government — management of government income, expenditure and debt
The management of income, expenditure and debt of an individual or family
The estimated list of income and expenditure of the government for a year
Budget where expenditure is more than income
The policy followed by the government in relation to its income and expenditure
A tax whose burden cannot be transferred — e.g., income tax, corporate tax
A tax whose burden can be transferred to consumers — e.g., GST
Higher tax on high-income group, lower tax on low-income group

mcqs.question: The financial year in India ends on
mcqs.explanation: India's financial year runs from April 1 to March 31

mcqs.question: The central budget is presented in the Lok Sabha by the
mcqs.explanation: The Union Finance Minister presents the central budget in the Lok Sabha

mcqs.question: Developing Countries usually present
mcqs.explanation: Developing countries usually present deficit budgets to fund development expenditure

mcqs.question: An example for direct tax is
mcqs.explanation: Corporate Tax is a direct tax — the burden cannot be transferred

one_mark_questions.question: What is budget?
one_mark_questions.answer: The estimated list of income and expenditure of the government for a year is called budget

one_mark_questions.question: What is deficit budget?
one_mark_questions.answer: If the expenditure is more than the income of the government in the budget, it is called deficit budget

one_mark_questions.question: What is direct tax?
one_mark_questions.answer: A tax whose burden cannot be transferred is called direct tax

one_mark_questions.question: What is progressive taxation?
one_mark_questions.answer: The government imposes higher rate of taxes on high-income group and lower rate on low-income group — called progressive taxation

three_mark_questions.question: What the differences between public finance and personal finance?
answer: Personal Finance: Related to income expenditure of an individual or family; expenditure made according to income; will be kept confidential
Public Finance: Related to income expenditure of government; revenue is earned according to expenditure; will be publicized; when government saves money, growth is stunned
